task_completion.description: Verify the agent successfully logged into the Rancher dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher dashboard is visible in the browser, showing the cluster overview or home screen.

task_completion.description: Verify the 'coredns' deployment in the 'kube-system' namespace is scaled to at least 1 replica.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Rancher UI, the 'coredns' deployment under the 'kube-system' namespace shows active replicas (e.g., 1/1 or 2/2) instead of being scaled to 0.

task_completion.description: Verify the CoreDNS ConfigMap is configured with the cluster domain 'cluster.local'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI showing the CoreDNS ConfigMap being edited or viewed, with the 'kubernetes' plugin configuration explicitly showing 'cluster.local'.

task_completion.description: Verify the CoreDNS ConfigMap is configured to forward external queries to a valid upstream resolver.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI showing the CoreDNS ConfigMap with the 'forward' plugin configured to a valid resolver (e.g., 'forward . /etc/resolv.conf' or an IP address like '8.8.8.8').

task_completion.description: Verify the 'kube-dns' service in the 'kube-system' namespace exposes standard DNS ports.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Rancher UI showing the 'kube-dns' service configuration or details, with port 53 exposed for both TCP and UDP protocols.

integrity.description: The agent used the Rancher GUI to perform the repairs rather than exclusively running kubectl commands in a terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the agent navigating, viewing, and editing resources within the Rancher web interface.

integrity.description: The agent actually applied the changes to the cluster resources.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the 'Save' or 'Update' buttons being clicked in Rancher, or the resources showing an 'Active' state after modification.

integrity.description: The agent did not simply type the expected configuration into a text editor to fake completion.
integrity.visual_evidence: Modifications are seen within the actual Rancher UI's resource editors (ConfigMap editor, Deployment scaler, Service port editor) rather than a standalone text editor.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target namespace is 'kube-system'. Target deployment to scale is 'coredns'. Target service to fix ports is 'kube-dns'. The agent must use the Rancher UI at https://localhost to perform these repairs.
